architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, base_model.py's Attention with the minimal delta of removing softmax (linear attention); solves y=α·x₁+β·x₂ exactly.

baseline_comparison.note: Softmax variant of the identical head run as strawman that collapses on |α|+|β|≠1 and negatives, plus the mean-target baseline; lift_over_baseline_r2=1.0.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built synthetic circuit (no trained model), explicitly stated; the linear-vs-softmax contrast is a clean causal manipulation but no model-level patching/ablation is proposed.

operating_range.note: Exact (R²=1.0) across the full 24-pair grid incl. negatives and magnitude-2 coeffs; algebraically scale-invariant but no extreme-scale (orders-of-magnitude) stress test shown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set Q/K/V/O with no training — the canonical hand-built circuit this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Two side-by-side R² heatmaps (linear vs softmax) on a shared scale plus a canonical pred-vs-target scatter make the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the α×β grid as the exact axis the robustness metric hinges on and the scatter as the smallest artefact that would bend if the mechanism were wrong.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built linear-attention head that computes y=α·x₁+β·x₂ exactly (R²=1.0, robustness=1.0, lift=1.0 over baseline across all 24 coefficient pairs). The softmax variant of the same head is an excellent strawman showing why dropping normalisation is what makes arbitrary-sign, magnitude>1 coefficients expressible, and the heatmap/scatter visualisation makes the claim immediately legible. Only soft spots are that, being purely synthetic, there is no model-level causal patching and no extreme-scale stress test — both acceptable and acknowledged for a hand-built circuit.
